full_suite.failure_notes: No failures. One warning from test_body_runner_real_path: FAST_SAM_PYTHON is unset, so BODY degrades from batched subprocess runtime to in-process fallback in this local environment.

honest_issues: This local repair proves production normalize_fast_sam_body_output applies pred_cam_t exactly once; it does not prove the historical W6 GATE-1b artifacts now pass because those raw sidecars must be regenerated or rerun on GPU.
Alternate skeleton-field quantification is still pending raw-sidecar evidence containing both pred_keypoints_3d and pred_joint_coords.
No BEST-STACK promotion is proposed yet. A PENDING decode-fidelity entry should wait for raw-sidecar GATE-1b recompute plus synthetic gate evidence.

The worktree remains dirty from other lanes before and after this repair, including protected/cross-lane files configs/racketsport/best_stack.json, scripts/racketsport/process_video.py, tests/racketsport/test_best_stack_manifest.py, tests/racketsport/test_process_video.py, and untracked labelpacks. I did not edit or revert them.
next: Run the GPU/raw-sidecar GATE-1b recompute on fresh BODY output that preserves pred_cam_t, pred_vertices, pred_keypoints_3d, and pred_joint_coords; then recompute alternate-field divergence and decide whether the residual is ceiling-rule family-normal or still a decode defect.
